item.dc:creator: Shannon Pettypiece
item.description: Under President Donald Trump’s “big, beautiful bill,” food stamp applicants are fighting to prove eligibility. A crisis in Arizona offers a warning for America.

media:content.media:text: People wait in line at St. Mary’s Food Bank, Surprise Neighborhood Food Center, Surprise, Ariz., on April 28th, 2026.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: Tiffany Hudson and son Jamie Hudson at their residence. Phoenix on April 28th, 2026.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: People wait in line at St. Mary’s Food Bank
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: SNAP in Arizona
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: Dawn Savinski, 62, checked in with a volunteer at St. Mary’s Food Bank.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: Enoch Asmah, 41, of Surprise, started volunteering at St. Mary’s Food Bank after coming to the U.S. from Ghana.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: Arizona SNAP
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: A volunteers at St. Mary’s Food Bank in Phoenix directs traffic the morning of April 29.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: There was a steady stream of cars throughout the morning of April 29 at St. Mary's Food Bank in Phoenix.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: Jena Hannon was among the more than 1,000 people who came to St. Mary’s Food Bank in Phoenix on April 29.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: The Arizona Department of Economic Security location in Peoria had a line of more than 30 people outside its doors when it opened April 29.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: Volunteers loaded food into one of the waiting cars at St. Mary’s Food Bank in Phoenix on April 29.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: Volunteers filled grocery carts with food to load into vehicles at St. Mary’s Food Bank in Phoenix.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

item.title: The families going hungry because of Trump’s food stamp cuts

item.dc:creator: Jonathan Allen
Peter Nicholas
Matt Dixon
Henry J. Gomez
Allan Smith
item.description: Some 4,000-plus spectators who are lucky enough, cunning enough or rich enough will score a seat to the UFC fight on the South Lawn next month.

media:content.media:text: A shiny, golden ticket that shows the UFC and White House logos with "ADMIT ONE" written on it.
Aerial view of a stadium with a UFC ring and TV screens showing President Donald Trump, Dana White and Hunter Campbel..
President Donald Trump waves to a cheering crowd.

item.title: The cage match is on for tickets to Trump’s UFC fight at the White House

item.description: President Trump appeared to reject Iran’s response to the latest U.S. peace proposal to end the war, calling the Iranian demands “totally unacceptable.” Iranian state media reported the response focuses on opening the Strait of Hormuz and ending the war, but does not mention nuclear weapons. NBC News’ Molly Hunter reports.

media:content.media:title: Trump calls Iran’s response to U.S. peace proposal ‘totally unacceptable’
media:content.media:description: President Trump appeared to reject Iran’s response to the latest U.S. peace proposal to end the war, calling the Iranian demands “totally unacceptable.” Iranian state media reported the response focuses on opening the Strait of Hormuz and ending the war, but does not mention nuclear weapons. NBC News’ Molly Hunter reports.

item.title: Trump calls Iran’s response to U.S. peace proposal ‘totally unacceptable’

item.description: Meet the Press Moderator Kristen Welker joins Sunday TODAY’s Willie Geist to discuss President Donald Trump’s high-stakes trip to meet with China’s Xi Jinping for the first time since the Iran war began. “China is the largest buyer of Iranian oil and has been backing Iran in this conflict. So to that end, Mr. Trump is expected to press Beijing to scale back that support and to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz,” Kristen says.

media:content.media:title: Trump Heads out on High-Stakes China Trip Amid Iran War
media:content.media:description: Meet the Press Moderator Kristen Welker joins Sunday TODAY’s Willie Geist to discuss President Donald Trump’s high-stakes trip to meet with China’s Xi Jinping for the first time since the Iran war began. “China is the largest buyer of Iranian oil and has been backing Iran in this conflict. So to that end, Mr. Trump is expected to press Beijing to scale back that support and to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz,” Kristen says.

item.title: Trump Heads out on High-Stakes China Trip Amid Iran War

item.description: The Americans evacuated from the deadly hantavirus cruise ship are on their way to the Nebraska Medical Center’s state of the art National Quarantine Unit. It is unclear how many of the Americans, who are all asymptomatic, will end up staying there and for how long. NBC News’ Camila Bernal has the latest.

media:content.media:title: Americans on hantavirus cruise ship head to Nebraska
media:content.media:description: The Americans evacuated from the deadly hantavirus cruise ship are on their way to the Nebraska Medical Center’s state of the art National Quarantine Unit. It is unclear how many of the Americans, who are all asymptomatic, will end up staying there and for how long. NBC News’ Camila Bernal has the latest.

item.title: Americans on hantavirus cruise ship head to Nebraska

item.description: Inside the facility monitoring Americans exposed to hantavirus

media:content.media:description: Inside the facility monitoring Americans exposed to hantavirus

item.title: Inside the facility monitoring Americans exposed to hantavirus

item.description: Nearly a year after President Donald Trump signed his “big, beautiful bill” into law, millions are losing SNAP benefits – and Arizona is becoming one of the clearest examples of the fallout. NBC News’ Shannon Pettypiece reports.

media:content.media:title: Arizona food banks swamped by SNAP cuts
media:content.media:description: Nearly a year after President Donald Trump signed his “big, beautiful bill” into law, millions are losing SNAP benefits – and Arizona is becoming one of the clearest examples of the fallout. NBC News’ Shannon Pettypiece reports.

item.title: Arizona food banks swamped by SNAP cuts

item.description: Sen. Cory Booker (D-N.J.) shared his opinion on the U.S. Supreme Court imposing new limits on the Voting Rights Act.

media:content.media:title: Senator Booker reacts to new SC ruling
media:content.media:description: Sen. Cory Booker (D-N.J.) shared his opinion on the U.S. Supreme Court imposing new limits on the Voting Rights Act.

item.title: Senator Booker reacts to new SC ruling

item.description: Passengers disembark from hantavirus-stricken cruise

media:content.media:description: Passengers disembark from hantavirus-stricken cruise

item.title: Passengers disembark from hantavirus-stricken cruise

item.description: As album rollouts continue to evolve, fans recently attempted to melt a giant ice block in the middle of Toronto as part of Drake's "Iceman" announcement. But how did we get here? NBC News' Gadi Schwartz takes a look back at other memorable rollouts, and how they have advanced through the years.

media:content.media:title: The evolution of album rollouts
media:content.media:description: As album rollouts continue to evolve, fans recently attempted to melt a giant ice block in the middle of Toronto as part of Drake's "Iceman" announcement. But how did we get here? NBC News' Gadi Schwartz takes a look back at other memorable rollouts, and how they have advanced through the years.

item.title: The evolution of album rollouts

item.dc:creator: Kayla Hayempour
David Hickey
item.description: In today’s newsletter: The 17 Americans who were aboard a hantavirus-stricken cruise ship have arrived in the U.S., one with mild symptoms.

media:content.media:text: People in haz-mat suits ascend a staircase into a jet at sunset.
media:content.media:description: Passengers of the MV Hondius board their respective flights to be transported to their countries of origin at the south airport of the island in Tenerife, Canary Islands, Spain on May 10, 2026.

media:content.media:text: Cuba US Women's Protest
media:content.media:description: Women attend a rally calling for the end of the U.S. blockade against the island nation in Havana, Cuba on April 7, 2026.

media:content.media:text: SNAP in Arizona
Spirit Airlines,
media:content.media:description: Aircraft parked out of service at Orlando International Airport.

item.title: Hantavirus cruise passengers arrive in U.S. and Trump’s frustration with Cuba: Morning Rundown

item.dc:creator: Jonathan Allen

Allan Smith

item.dc:creator: Mithil Aggarwal
Janis Mackey Frayer
item.description: U.S. and Chinese authorities jointly arrested five suspects in a cross-border smuggling and trafficking operation, Beijing said Monday, touting the cooperation ahead of President Donald Trump’s state visit this week.

media:content.media:text: President Donald Trump talks to China's President Xi Jinping after their talks at the Gimhae Air Base in Busan, South Korea, on October 30, 2025.
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump talks to China's President Xi Jinping after their talks at the Gimhae Air Base in Busan, South Korea, on October 30, 2025.

item.title: U.S. and China arrest 5 in joint drug smuggling investigation ahead of Trump trip

item.dc:creator: Patrick Smith
item.description: The 17 Americans who were aboard the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship Hondius have now arrived home in the United States

media:content.media:text: First passengers leave HV Hondius amid suspected hantavirus concerns in Tenerife
media:content.media:description: The first passengers from the MV Hondius depart for Tenerife Airport aboard a Spanish Military Emergency Unit bus, escorted by a member of Spainâs External Health Service, after disembarking at Granadilla Port in Tenerife, Canary Islands, on May 10, 2026.

item.title: American passengers from hantavirus-hit cruise ship arrive back in the U.S.
